resources.name: Action types
resources.description:
Action types are the types of actions that can be created for a specific connection. They differ based on the target they operate on:
* `Events` for actions that receive or send events
* `Users` for actions that import or export users
* `Groups` for actions that import or export groups

If the connection is a destination, the actions on events may also vary depending on the type of event.

endpoints.name: List action types for a connection
endpoints.description: Retrieves the action types for a connection.

parameters.name: :id
parameters.description: The ID of the connection for which the action types are returned.

parameters.name: actionTypes

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the action type.

properties.name: description
properties.placeholder: "Export the users  as contacts to Mailchimp"
properties.description: The description of the action type.

properties.name: target
properties.description: The target of the action.

properties.name: eventType
properties.description: The event type of the action. It is null if target is not `"Users"` or `"Groups"`.

parameters.description: The action types of the connection.

resources.name: Connection app
resources.description: A connection enables Meergo to retrieve customer and event data from an external source location or send them to an external destination location.

endpoints.name: List the app users
endpoints.description: Returns the users of an app.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: The schema that the returned users must satisfy.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The cursor used to fetch the next set of users. If empty, it returns the first set of users.

parameters.name: users
parameters.description: The app users from the provided cursor.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The cursor to be passed to retrieve the next set of users. If there are no more users, the cursor will be empty.

errors.message: schema is not aligned with the app's source schema

endpoints.name: Preview send event
endpoints.description:
Returns a preview of an event as it would be sent to the connection's app.

The connection must be a destination app connection, and it is expected to have the provided event type.

parameters.name: evenType
parameters.description: The event type.

parameters.name: event
parameters.description: The event that would be sent. It must conform to the schema of the event type.

parameters.name: transformation
parameters.description: The transformation.

parameters.name: outSchema
parameters.description: The output schema.

errors.message: connection does not have the event type
output schema is not compatible with the event type's schema

endpoints.name: Get the user schemas
endpoints.description: Returns the source and destination user schema of an app connection.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the app connection.

parameters.name: sourceSchema
parameters.description: The source user schema of the app connection.

parameters.name: destinationSchema
parameters.description: The destination user schema of the app connection.

endpoints.name: Get the group schemas
endpoints.description: Returns the source and destination group schema of an app connection.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the app connection.

parameters.name: sourceSchema
parameters.description: The source group schema of the app connection.

parameters.name: destinationSchema
parameters.description: The destination group schema of the app connection.

endpoints.name: Get an event type schema
endpoints.description: Returns the schema of an event type within a destination app connection.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the destination app connection.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: The schema of the event type.

endpoints.name: Retrieve a database query schema
endpoints.description: It executes the provided query on the source database connection and returns the result schema along with sample rows for reference.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the source database connection on which the query will be executed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The query to execute on the connection's database to read the result schema along with sample rows for reference. It must contain the "limit" placeholder and cannot be longer than 16,777,215 characters.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of rows to return along with the schema. The maximum is 100; the default is 0, meaning no rows are returned.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: The schema of the query.

parameters.name: rows
parameters.description: The rows.

endpoints.name: Retrieve a database table schema
endpoints.description: Returns the schema of a specified database table of a destination database connection.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The database destination connection where the table is located.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the table.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: The schema of the table.

resources.name: Connection files
resources.description: A connection enables Meergo to retrieve customer and event data from an external source location or send them to an external destination location.

endpoints.name: Retrive a file
endpoints.description: Returns schema and first rows of a file.

parameters.name: connection
parameters.description: The file storage source connection from which to read the file.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description:
The file path relative to the root of the connection. For details on the required format, refer to the file storage connector documentation.

The path must not be empty and cannot exceed 1024 characters in length.

Note that the path must be URL-encoded. For example, if the path is `docs/users/subscribers.csv`, you need to pass it as `docs%2Fusers%2Fsubscribers.csv`.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The file format. It correspond to the name of a file connector.

parameters.name: sheet
parameters.description:
The sheet name. It can only be used with the Excel format, where it is required.

When provided, it must have a length between 1 and 31 characters, not start or end with a single quote `'`, and cannot contain any of the following characters: `*`, `/`, `:`, `?`, `[`, `\`, and `]`.

parameters.name: compression
parameters.description:
The method used to compress the file, if applicable.

**Note:** An Excel file is inherently compressed, so no compression method needs to be specified unless the file has been further compressed.

parameters.name: formatSettings
parameters.description:
The settings for the file format. Refer to the documentation for the [connector](/connectors/) related to the file format to understand the available settings and their corresponding values.

If the file format does not require any settings, the `formatSettings` field may be omitted or set to null.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of rows to return along with the schema. The maximum is 100; the default is 0, meaning no rows are returned.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: The file's schema.

parameters.name: rows
parameters.description: The rows.

endpoints.name: Read the sheet names
endpoints.description: Returns the list of sheets in a file, applicable to file formats that support sheets.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The file storage source connection from which to read the file.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description:
The file path relative to the root of the connection. For details on the required format, refer to the file storage connector documentation.

The path must not be empty and cannot exceed 1024 characters in length.

Note that the path must be URL-encoded. For example, if the path is `docs/users/subscribers.csv`, you need to pass it as `docs%2Fusers%2Fsubscribers.csv`.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The file format. It correspond to the name of a file connector.

parameters.name: compression
parameters.description:
The method used to compress the file, if applicable.

**Note:** An Excel file is inherently compressed, so no compression method needs to be specified unless the file has been further compressed.

parameters.name: formatSettings
parameters.description:
The settings for the file format. Refer to the documentation for the [connector](/connectors/) related to the file format to understand the available settings and their corresponding values.

If the file format does not require any settings, the `formatSettings` field may be omitted or set to null.

parameters.name: sheets
parameters.description: The file's sheets. They have a length between 1 and 31 characters, not start or end with a single quote `'`, and cannot contain any of the following characters: `*`, `/`, `:`, `?`, `[`, `\`, and `]`.

endpoints.name: Retrive the absolute path
endpoints.description:
Returns the absolute path of a file based on its path relative to the connection’s root.

While this complete path isn’t used directly by other API endpoints, it can help confirm that the relative path points to the correct file.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the file storage connection for which the path should be retrieved.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description:
The file path relative to the root of the connection. For details on the required format, refer to the file storage connector documentation.

The path must not be empty and cannot exceed 1024 characters in length.

Note that the path must be URL-encoded. For example, if the path is `docs/users/subscribers.csv`, you need to pass it as `docs%2Fusers%2Fsubscribers.csv`.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The complete path of the file.

resources.name: Connection identities
resources.description: Identities are the users obtained from sources like apps, databases, files, or events. These identities are filtered and transformed according to a defined action. Once identity resolution is complete, the collected identities from all connections are unified to form the workspace users.

endpoints.name: List the connection identities
endpoints.description: Retrieves the identities associated with a connection.

parameters.name: connection
parameters.description: The source connection from which to retrive the identities.

parameters.name: first
parameters.description: The number of identities to skip before starting to return results. The default value is 0.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of identities to return. The value must be within the range [1, 1000].

parameters.name: identities
parameters.description: The connection's identities.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: The estimated total number of identities in the connection.

errors.message: data warehouse is in maintenance mode
error occurred with the data warehouse

resources.name: Connection keys
resources.description:
The connection keys are used for authentication when sending events to Meergo.

Mobile and server keys are private, while website keys are usually public, as they can be accessed from the site’s source code.
A connection can have at most 20 keys.

endpoints.name: Create a connection key
endpoints.description: Creates a key for a mobile, server, or website source connection. Returns an error if the connection already has the maximum limit of 20 keys.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of a mobile, server, or website connection.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The new key of the connection.

endpoints.name: List all connection keys
endpoints.description: Returns all keys for mobile, server, and website source connections.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of a mobile, server, or website connection.

parameters.name: keys
parameters.description: The keys of the connection.

endpoints.name: Delete a connection key
endpoints.description: Delete a key of a mobile, server, or website source connection.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of a mobile, server, or website connection.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The key to delete.

errors.message: key cannot be deleted as it is the connection’s only key

resources.name: Connections
resources.description: A connection enables Meergo to retrieve customer and event data from an external source location or send them to an external destination location.

endpoints.name: Create a connection
endpoints.description: Create a new connection.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The connection's name.

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: Indicates if the connection is a source or a destination.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Indicate if the connection is enabled.

parameters.name: connector
parameters.description: The name of the [connector](/connectors/) for which to create the connection. It can be an app, database, file storage, mobile, server, stream, or website connector, but cannot be a file connector.

parameters.name: strategy
parameters.description: The [strategy](/identity-resolution/anonymous-users-strategies) for anonymous users. It is required and can only be provided for source mobile and website connections.

parameters.name: sendingMode
parameters.description: The mode for sending events. It is required and can only be provided with destination app connections that support it. In this case, it must be one of the sending modes supported by the app.

parameters.name: websiteHost
parameters.description: The host of the website. It is used for documentation purposes only, with no functional impact.

parameters.name: linkedConnections
parameters.description:
The connections (IDs) to which to send or from which to receive events.

For source mobile, server, or website connections, linked connections are the app connections to which the received events are sent. On the other hand, for destination app connections, linked connections are the source mobile, website, and server connections from which events are received.

If it is null, there are no linked connections, or the connection is not one of the mentioned types.

parameters.name: settings
parameters.description:
The specific settings of the connection, which vary based on the connector specified in the `connector` field. Please refer to the documentation for the [connector](/connectors/) to understand the available settings and their corresponding values.

If the connector does not require any settings, the `settings` field may be omitted or set to null.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the connection.

endpoints.name: Get an OAuth token
endpoints.description: Retrieves an OAuth token that can be used to create a connection that requires OAuth authentication.

parameters.name: oauthCode
parameters.description: The OAuth authorization code to complete the authentication process.

parameters.name: redirectURI
parameters.description: The URI where the user will be redirected after authorization.

parameters.name: connector
parameters.description: The name of the connector for which the connection will be created.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: The OAuth token that can be used to create a connection on the specified connector.

endpoints.name: Update a connection
endpoints.description: Update a connection.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the connection.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The connection's name.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Indicate if the connection is enabled.

parameters.name: strategy
parameters.description: The [strategy](/identity-resolution/anonymous-users-strategies) for anonymous users. It is required and can only be provided for source mobile and website connections.

parameters.name: sendingMode
parameters.description: The mode for sending events. It is required and can only be provided with destination app connections that support it. In this case, it must be one of the sending modes supported by the app.

parameters.name: websiteHost
parameters.description: The host of the website. It is used for documentation purposes only, with no functional impact.

endpoints.name: List all connections
endpoints.description: Returns the workspace's connections, sorted by name.

properties.name: id
properties.description: The ID of the connection.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The connection's name.

properties.name: type
properties.description: The type of the connection's connector.

properties.name: role
properties.description: Indicates if the connection is a source or a destination.

properties.name: enabled
properties.description: Indicate if the connection is enabled.

properties.name: connector
properties.description: The name of the connection's [connector](/connectors/).

properties.name: strategy
properties.description: The [strategy](/identity-resolution/anonymous-users-strategies) for anonymous users. It is `null` if the connection is not a mobile or website source connection.

properties.name: sendingMode
properties.description: The mode for sending events. It is null if the connection is not a destination app connection that supports sending mode.

properties.name: websiteHost
properties.description: The host of the website. It is null if the connection is not a website connection. It is used for documentation purposes only, with no functional impact.

properties.name: linkedConnections
properties.description:
The connections (IDs) to which to send or from which to receive events.

For source mobile, server, or website connections, linked connections are the app connections to which the received events are sent. On the other hand, for destination app connections, linked connections are the source mobile, website, and server connections from which events are received.

If it is null, there are no linked connections, or the connection is not one of the mentioned types.

properties.name: hasSettings
properties.description: It indicates if the connection has settings.

properties.name: actionsCount
properties.description: The total number of actions of the connection.

properties.name: health
properties.description: The connection's healthy.

resources.name: Connectors
resources.description: Connectors allows to instantiate [connections](connections) to interface Meergo with external data.

endpoints.name: List all connectors
endpoints.description: Returns the connectors, sorted by name.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The connector's name.

properties.name: sourceDescription
properties.placeholder: "import contacts as users and companies as groups from HubSpot"
properties.description: A brief description of the connector when it is used as a source. It complete the sentence "Add an action to ...".

properties.name: destinationDescription
properties.placeholder: "export users as contacts and groups as companies to HubSpot"
properties.description: A brief description of the connector when it is used as a destination. It should complete the sentence "Add an action to ...".

properties.name: termForUsers
properties.description: The term used by an app to indicate the users. For example "clients", "customers" or "users". It will be empty if the connector is not an app or if the app does not handle users.

properties.name: termForGroups
properties.description:
The term used by an app to indicate the groups. For example "organizations", "teams", or "groups".

It will be empty if the connector is not an app or if the app does not handle groups.

properties.name: type
properties.description: The type of connector.

properties.name: targets
properties.description:
The targets supported by the app connector. It includes one or more of the following: `"Events"`, `"Users"`, and `"Groups"`.

It will be empty if the connector is not an app.

properties.name: sendingMode
properties.description: The mode used by app connectors to dispatch the events to the app, if the app supports events. It is empty is the connector is not an app or it does not handle events.

properties.name: hasSheets
properties.description: It indicates, for file connectors, if it supports sheets. It is false if the connector is not a file connector or does not support sheets.

properties.name: hasSettings
properties.description: It indicates if the connector has settings.

properties.name: identityIDLabel
properties.description:
The descriptive name of the identifier used by the app to identify a user. For example "ID", "User ID", or "HubSpot ID".

It is empty if the connector is not an app.

properties.name: icon
properties.description:
The icon in SVG format representing the connector, minimized for embedding in an HTML page.

It is empty if the connector does not have an icon.

properties.name: fileExtension
properties.description:
The main extension of the file type that the connector reads and writes. It is used as a placeholder in the input field, where the user specifies the file name to read or write.

It is empty if the connector is not a file connector.

properties.name: sampleQuery
properties.description:
The sample query displayed in the query editor when creating a new database source action.

It is empty if the connector is not a database connector.

properties.name: webhooksPer
properties.description:
"Indicates, for app connectors supporting webhooks, whether webhooks are per account, connection, or connector.

It is `"None"` if the connector is not an app or does not support webhooks.

properties.name: oAuth
properties.description: Indicates, for app connections, whether it supports authentication with OAuth 2.0. It is false if the connector is not an app or does not support OAuth.

endpoints.name: Get a connector
endpoints.description: Get a connector by name.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The connector's name.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The connector's name.

parameters.name: sourceDescription
parameters.placeholder: "import contacts as users and companies as groups from HubSpot"
parameters.description: A brief description of the connector when it is used as a source. It complete the sentence "Add an action to ...".

parameters.name: destinationDescription
parameters.placeholder: "export users as contacts and groups as companies to HubSpot"
parameters.description: A brief description of the connector when it is used as a destination. It should complete the sentence "Add an action to ...".

parameters.name: termForUsers
parameters.description: The term used by an app to indicate the users. For example "clients", "customers" or "users". It will be empty if the connector is not an app or if the app does not handle users.

parameters.name: termForGroups
parameters.description:
The term used by an app to indicate the groups. For example "organizations", "teams", or "groups".

It will be empty if the connector is not an app or if the app does not handle groups.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The type of connector.

parameters.name: targets
parameters.description:
The targets supported by the app connector. It includes one or more of the following: `"Events"`, `"Users"`, and `"Groups"`.

It will be empty if the connector is not an app.

parameters.name: sendingMode
parameters.description: The mode used by app connectors to dispatch the events to the app, if the app supports events. It is empty is the connector is not an app or it does not handle events.

parameters.name: hasSheets
parameters.description: It indicates, for file connectors, if it supports sheets. It is false if the connector is not a file connector or does not support sheets.

parameters.name: hasSettings

parameters.name: identityIDLabel
parameters.description:
The descriptive name of the identifier used by the app to identify a user. For example "ID", "User ID", or "HubSpot ID".

It is empty if the connector is not an app.

parameters.name: icon
parameters.description:
The icon in SVG format representing the connector, minimized for embedding in an HTML page.

It is empty if the connector does not have an icon.

parameters.name: fileExtension
parameters.description:
The main extension of the file type that the connector reads and writes. It is used as a placeholder in the input field, where the user specifies the file name to read or write.

It is empty if the connector is not a file connector.

parameters.name: sampleQuery
parameters.description:
The sample query displayed in the query editor when creating a new database source action.

It is empty if the connector is not a database connector.

parameters.name: webhooksPer
parameters.description:
"Indicates, for app connectors supporting webhooks, whether webhooks are per account, connection, or connector.

It is `"None"` if the connector is not an app or does not support webhooks.

parameters.name: oAuth
parameters.description: Indicates, for app connections, whether it supports authentication with OAuth 2.0. It is false if the connector is not an app or does not support OAuth.

endpoints.name: Get OAuth consent URL
endpoints.description: Gets the URL for an app connector that directs to the consent page of the app's OAuth 2.0 provider.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The connector's name. It must be an app connector that supports authorization with OAuth.

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: The role for which to request authorization.

parameters.name: redirecturi
parameters.description: The URL to which redirect after granting permissions.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The OAuth consent URL that directs to the consent page of the app's OAuth 2.0 provider. This page requests explicit permissions for the scopes required by the role.

resources.name: Destination actions on events
resources.description: A destination action on events is an action that streams events received from a source connection to an app in real time.

endpoints.name: Create a destination action on events
endpoints.description: Create a new destination action on events.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The action's name.

parameters.name: connection
parameters.description: The connection for which the action should be created. It should be a destination app connection that support events.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Indicate if the action is enabled once created.

parameters.name: filter

properties.name: conditions
property
properties.description: The name or path of the property. If the property has a JSON type, it can include a JSON path.

properties.name: operator

type.name: Text
values: is less than or equal to
is greater than or equal to

properties.description: The condition's operator. The allowed values depend on the property's type.

properties.name: values
properties.description: The values the operator applies to, if any. These depend on both the operator and the property's type, including whether they're present and how many there are.
A filter's condition.

parameters.description:
The filter applied to the events. If it's not null, only the events that match the filter will be sent to the destination.

See the [filters documentation](/filters) for more details.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the action.

endpoints.description: Update a destination action on events.

endpoints.name: List all events
endpoints.description: This endpoint retrieves events stored in the workspace's data warehouse, up to a maximum number of events defined by `limit`. You must specify which properties to include. If a filter is provided, only events that match the filter criteria will be returned.

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: The event properties to return.

parameters.name: filter

properties.name: conditions
property
properties.description: The name or path of the property. If the property has a JSON type, it can include a JSON path.

properties.name: operator

type.name: Text
values: is less than or equal to
is greater than or equal to

properties.description: The condition's operator. The allowed values depend on the property's type.

properties.name: values
properties.description: The values the operator applies to, if any. These depend on both the operator and the property's type, including whether they're present and how many there are.
A filter's condition.

parameters.description: The filter applied to the events. If it's not null, only the events that match the filter will be returned.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The property by which to sort the events to be returned.

parameters.name: orderDesc
parameters.description: The descending sorting order in which to return the events: if true, the events are sorted in descending order; otherwise, they are sorted in ascending order.

endpoints.name: Get the event schema
endpoints.description: Return the event schema. The event schema is the same for all workspaces.

endpoints.name: Create an event listener
endpoints.description: Creates an event listener to the workspace that listens to events and returns its identifier.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The maximum number of observed events to return. It must be between 1 and 1000. If not specified or set to null, the default is 10.

parameters.name: filter

properties.name: conditions
property
properties.description: The name or path of the property. If the property has a JSON type, it can include a JSON path.

properties.name: operator

type.name: Text
values: is less than or equal to
is greater than or equal to

properties.description: The condition's operator. The allowed values depend on the property's type.

properties.name: values
properties.description: The values the operator applies to, if any. These depend on both the operator and the property's type, including whether they're present and how many there are.
A filter's condition.

parameters.description: The filter applied to the events. If not null, only events that match the filter will be included.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the event listener.

errors.message: there are already the maximum of number of listeners

endpoints.name: Retrive observed events
endpoints.description: Returns the events captured by the specified listener along with the count of discarded events.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the event listener.

parameters.name: events
parameters.description: The observed events.

parameters.name: discarded
parameters.description: The number of discarded events.

endpoints.name: Delete an event listener
endpoints.description: Deletes an event listener. It does nothing if the event lister does not exist.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the event listener.

resources.name: Executions
resources.description: The executions refer to action executions. User actions are automatically triggered by the scheduler or can be started by calling the specific endpoint for the action to execute.

endpoints.name: List all action executions
endpoints.description: Returns all action executions.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the execution.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: The ID of the executed action.

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: The start time in ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: The end time in ISO 8601 format. It is null if the execution has not yet finished.

parameters.name: passed
parameters.description: The number of passed users or events.

parameters.name: failed
parameters.description: The number of failed users or events.

parameters.name: error
parameters.description: An error occurred during execution, causing it to stop prematurely. It is empty if the execution has not yet finished or if no error occurred.

resources.name: Identity resolution
resources.description: Identities are the users obtained from sources like apps, databases, files, or events. These identities are filtered and transformed according to a defined action. Once identity resolution is complete, the collected identities from all connections are unified to form the workspace users.

parameters.name: runOnBatchImport
parameters.description: Indicates if identity resolution is automatically run when a batch import is completed. By default is false.

parameters.name: identifiers
parameters.description: The identifiers in the specified order. An identifier must be a property in the user schema with a type of `Int`, `Uint`, `UUID`, `Inet`, `Text`, or `Decimal` with zero scale. Identifiers cannot be repeated.

errors.message: property does not exist in the user schema
a property has a type which is not allowed for identifiers

parameters.name: runOnBatchImport
parameters.description: Indicates if identity resolution is automatically run when a batch import is completed. By default is false.

parameters.name: identifiers
parameters.description: The identifiers in the specified order.

endpoints.name: Start identity resolution
endpoints.description: Starts identity resolution that resolves the identities of the workspace.

errors.message: data warehouse is in inspection mode
data warehouse is in maintenance mode

endpoints.name: Retrieve last identity resolution info
endpoints.description: Returns the start and end times of the last identity resolution.

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: The UTC start time of the latest identity resolution. It is null if no identity resolution has been executed yet.

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: The UTC end time of the latest identity resolution. It is null if an identity resolution is still in progress or if none has been executed yet.

errors.message: data warehouse is in maintenance mode
error occurred with the data warehouse

resources.name: Linked connections
resources.description: When a source connection is linked to a destination connection, events received from the source are forwarded to the destination.

endpoints.name: Link connections
endpoints.description: Links a source connection to a destination connection and vice versa.

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: The ID of a source mobile, server, or website connection.

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description: The ID of a destination app connection that handle events.

endpoints.name: Unlink connections
endpoints.description: Unlink a source connection from a destination connection and vice versa.

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: The ID of a source mobile, server, or website connection.

parameters.name: dst
parameters.description: The ID of a destination app connection that handle events.

resources.name: Metrics and errors
resources.description: Metrics and errors related to the actions, including both import/export processes and the sending or receiving of events within the pipelines.

endpoints.name: Get action metrics per dates
endpoints.description: Retrieves metrics for actions aggregated by day for a time interval between specified start and end dates.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description:
The starting date in ISO 8601 format.

The date must be no earlier than 1970-01-01T00:00:00 and no later than the 2262-04-11T23:47:00, and the date must be earlier than the end date.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description:
The ending date in ISO 8601 format.

The date must be no earlier than 1970-01-01T00:00:00 and no later than the 2262-04-11T23:47:00, and the date must be later than the start date.

parameters.name: actions
parameters.description:
The IDs of the actions for which metrics should be returned. At least one action must be provided. The request does not fail if an action does not exist within the workspace.

The actions can be specified in the query string in two ways:
* `actions=705981339,1360924687`
* `actions=705981339&actions=1360924687`

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: The starting date in the `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss` ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: The ending date in the `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss` ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: passed
parameters.description: The number of users or events that successfully passed each step on each hour within the start and end dates.

parameters.name: failed
parameters.description: The number of users or events that failed at each step on each hour within the start and end dates.

endpoints.name: Get action metrics per day
endpoints.description: Retrieves metrics for actions for a specified number of days up to the current time.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: The number of days, ranging from 1 to 30. By default, it is 30.

parameters.name: actions
parameters.description:
The IDs of the actions for which metrics should be returned. At least one action must be provided. The request does not fail if an action does not exist within the workspace.

The actions can be specified in the query string in two ways:
* `actions=705981339,1360924687`
* `actions=705981339&actions=1360924687`

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: The starting date in the `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss` ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: The ending date in the `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss` ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: passed
parameters.description: The number of users or events that successfully passed each step on each hour within the start and end dates.

parameters.name: failed
parameters.description: The number of users or events that failed at each step on each hour within the start and end dates.

endpoints.name: Get action metrics per hour
endpoints.description: Retrieves metrics for actions for a specified number of hours up to the current time.

parameters.name: hours
parameters.description: The number of hours, ranging from 1 to 48. By default, it is 48.

parameters.name: actions
parameters.description:
The IDs of the actions for which metrics should be returned. At least one action must be provided. The request does not fail if an action does not exist within the workspace.

The actions can be specified in the query string in two ways:
* `actions=705981339,1360924687`
* `actions=705981339&actions=1360924687`

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: The starting date in the `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss` ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: The ending date in the `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss` ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: passed
parameters.description: The number of users or events that successfully passed each step on each hour within the start and end dates.

parameters.name: failed
parameters.description: The number of users or events that failed at each step on each hour within the start and end dates.

endpoints.name: Get action metrics per minute
endpoints.description: Retrieves metrics for actions for a specified number of minutes up to the current time.

parameters.name: minutes
parameters.description: The number of minutes, ranging from 1 to 60. By default, it is 60.

parameters.name: actions
parameters.description:
The IDs of the actions for which metrics should be returned. At least one action must be provided. The request does not fail if an action does not exist within the workspace.

The actions can be specified in the query string in two ways:
* `actions=705981339,1360924687`
* `actions=705981339&actions=1360924687`

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: The starting date in the `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss` ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: The ending date in the `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss` ISO 8601 format.

parameters.name: passed
parameters.description: The number of users or events that successfully passed each step on each hour within the start and end dates.

parameters.name: failed
parameters.description: The number of users or events that failed at each step on each hour within the start and end dates.

endpoints.name: Get action errors
endpoints.description: Retrieves errors for actions for a time interval between specified start and end dates.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description:
The starting date in ISO 8601 format.

The date must be no earlier than 1970-01-01T00:00:00 and no later than the 2262-04-11T23:47:00, and the date must be earlier than the end date.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description:
The ending date in ISO 8601 format.

The date must be no earlier than 1970-01-01T00:00:00 and no later than the 2262-04-11T23:47:00, and the date must be later than the start date.

parameters.name: actions
parameters.description:
The IDs of the actions for which errors should be returned. At least one action must be provided. The request does not fail if an action does not exist within the workspace.

The actions can be specified in the query string in two ways:
* `actions=705981339,1360924687`
* `actions=705981339&actions=1360924687`

parameters.name: step
parameters.description: The pipeline step for which errors should be returned. If no step is specified, errors will be returned for all steps.

parameters.name: first
parameters.description: The number of errors to skip before starting to return errors. It must be between 0 and 9999, with a default value of 0.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of errors to return. It must be between 0 and 100, with a default value of 100.

parameters.name: errors

properties.name: action
properties.description: The action in which the error occurred.

properties.name: step
properties.description: The step where the error occurred.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of occurrences of the error within the specified period.

properties.name: message
properties.description: The error message.

properties.name: lastOccurred
properties.description: The date of the last occurrence of the error within the specified period.

parameters.description: The errors that occurred during the specified period for the provided actions.

resources.name: Source actions on events
resources.description: A source action on events is an action that ingests incoming event data—from a website, mobile app, or server connection—and loads it into the workspace's data warehouse for storage and processing.

endpoints.description: Create a new source action on events.

parameters.description: The action's name.

parameters.name: connection

parameters.name: enabled

parameters.name: filter

properties.name: conditions
property
properties.description: The name or path of the property. If the property has a JSON type, it can include a JSON path.

type.name: Text
values: is less than or equal to
is greater than or equal to

properties.description: The condition's operator. The allowed values depend on the property's type.

properties.name: values
properties.description: The values the operator applies to, if any. These depend on both the operator and the property's type, including whether they're present and how many there are.
A filter's condition.

parameters.description:
The filter applied to the events. If it's not null, only the events that match the filter will be received.

See the [filters documentation](/filters) for more details.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the action.

parameters.description:
The schedule period in minutes.

Possible values: `5`, `15`, `30`, `60`, `120`, `180`, `360`, `480`, `720`, `1440`.

endpoints.name: Import users from an app
endpoints.description:
Starts a source app action execution to import its users into the workspace’s data warehouse, applying the action's filter and transformation.

It returns immediately without waiting for the execution to complete. To track the progress, call the [`/executions/:id`](/api/executions) endpoint using the returned execution ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the source app action on users.

parameters.name: reload
parameters.description: Indicates whether the users should be re-imported from scratch. If set to false or omitted, only new users and those modified since the last import are processed.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the started execution.

errors.message: data warehouse is in inspection mode
data warehouse is in maintenance mode

endpoints.name: Delete a source app action on users
endpoints.description: Delete a source app action on users.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the source app action on users.

resources.name: Source file actions
resources.description: A source file action is an action that extracts user data from a file and loads it into the workspace data warehouse for further processing and analysis.

endpoints.name: Create a source file action
endpoints.description: Create a source file action.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The action's name.

parameters.name: connection
parameters.description: The connection for which the action should be created. It should be a **source file storage** connection.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Indicate if the action is enabled once created.

parameters.name: filter

properties.name: conditions
property
properties.description: The name or path of the property. If the property has a JSON type, it can include a JSON path.

properties.name: operator

type.name: Text
values: is less than or equal to
is greater than or equal to

properties.description: The condition's operator. The allowed values depend on the property's type.

properties.name: values
properties.description: The values the operator applies to, if any. These depend on both the operator and the property's type, including whether they're present and how many there are.
A filter's condition.

parameters.description:
The filter applied to the users in the file. If it's not null, only the users that match the filter will be included.

See the [filters documentation](/filters) for more details.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The file format. It correspond to the name of a file connector.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The file path relative to the root path defined in the action's connection. Refer to the file storage connector documentation for details on the specific format.

parameters.name: sheet
parameters.description:
The sheet name. It can only be used with the Excel format, where it is required.

When provided, it must have a length between 1 and 31 characters, not start or end with a single quote `'`, and cannot contain any of the following characters: `*`, `/`, `:`, `?`, `[`, `\`, and `]`.

parameters.name: compression
parameters.description:
The method used to compress the file, if applicable.

**Note:** An Excel file is inherently compressed, so no compression method needs to be specified unless the file has been further compressed.

parameters.name: formatSettings
parameters.description:
The settings for the file format. Refer to the documentation for the [connector](/connectors/) related to the file format to understand the available settings and their corresponding values.

If the file format does not require any settings, the `formatSettings` field may be omitted or set to null.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the action.

endpoints.description: Update a source file action.

properties.description: The condition's operator. The allowed values depend on the property's type.

properties.name: values
properties.description: The values the operator applies to, if any. These depend on both the operator and the property's type, including whether they're present and how many there are.
A filter's condition.

parameters.description:
The filter applied to the users in the file. If it's not null, only the users that match the filter will be included.

See the [filters documentation](/filters) for more details.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The file format. It correspond to the name of a file connector.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The file path relative to the root path defined in the action's connection. Refer to the file storage connector documentation for details on the specific format.

parameters.name: sheet
parameters.description:
The sheet name. It can only be used with the Excel format, where it is required.

When provided, it must have a length between 1 and 31 characters, not start or end with a single quote `'`, and cannot contain any of the following characters: `*`, `/`, `:`, `?`, `[`, `\`, and `]`.

parameters.name: compression
parameters.description:
The method used to compress the file, if applicable.

**Note:** An Excel file is inherently compressed, so no compression method needs to be specified unless the file has been further compressed.

endpoints.name: Import users from a file
endpoints.description:
Starts a source file action execution to import users from the action's file into the workspace’s data warehouse, applying the action's transformation.

It returns immediately without waiting for the execution to complete. To track the progress, call the [`/executions/:id`](/api/executions) endpoint using the returned execution ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the source file action.

parameters.name: reload
parameters.description: Indicates whether the users should be re-imported from scratch. If set to false or omitted, only new users and those modified since the last import are processed.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the started execution.

errors.message: data warehouse is in inspection mode
data warehouse is in maintenance mode

endpoints.name: Delete a source file action
endpoints.description: Delete a source file action.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the source file action.

endpoints.name: List supported languages
endpoints.description: Returns a list of supported languages that can be used for transformation functions.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the supported language.

properties.name: icon
properties.description: The icon of the supported language.

endpoints.name: List all users
endpoints.description: This endpoint retrieves users stored in the workspace's data warehouse, up to a maximum number of users defined by `limit`. You must specify which properties to include. If a filter is provided, only users that match the filter criteria will be returned.

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: The user properties to return.

parameters.name: filter

properties.name: conditions
property
properties.description: The name or path of the property. If the property has a JSON type, it can include a JSON path.

properties.name: operator

type.name: Text
values: is less than or equal to
is greater than or equal to

properties.description: The condition's operator. The allowed values depend on the property's type.

properties.name: values
properties.description: The values the operator applies to, if any. These depend on both the operator and the property's type, including whether they're present and how many there are.
A filter's condition.

parameters.description: The filter applied to the users. If it's not null, only the users that match the filter will be returned.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The property by which to sort the users to be returned. It can be any property from the user schema with an sortable type, meaning it cannot be of type JSON, Array, Object, or Map.

parameters.name: orderDesc
parameters.description: The descending sorting order in which to return the users: if true, the users are sorted in descending order; otherwise, they are sorted in ascending order.

parameters.name: first
parameters.description: The number of users to skip before starting to return results. The default value is 0.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of users to return. The value must be within the range [1, 1000].

errors.message: data warehouse is in maintenance mode
order does not exist in schema
property does not exist in the user schema
error occurred with the data warehouse

endpoints.name: Retrive the traits of a user
endpoints.description: Returns the traits of a user given its identifier.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the user.

parameters.name: traits
parameters.description: The traits of the user, following the user schema.

errors.message: data warehouse is in maintenance mode
error occurred with the data warehouse

endpoints.name: Retrive the identities of a user
endpoints.description: Returns the identities of a user given its identifier.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the user.

parameters.name: first
parameters.description: The number of identities to skip before starting to return results. The default value is 0.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of identities to return. It must be between 0 and 1000, with a default value of 1000.

parameters.name: identities

properties.name: connection
properties.description: The ID of the connection through which the identity was observed.

properties.name: action
properties.description: The ID of the action through which the identity was observed.

properties.name: id
properties.description: The ID of the identity. It is empty for identities imported from anonymous events.

properties.name: anonymousIds
properties.description: The anonymousIds of the identity. It is null for identities not imported from events.

properties.name: lastChangeTime
properties.description: The identity’s most recent change time.

parameters.description: The user’s identities, containing at least one identity.

errors.message: data warehouse is in maintenance mode
error occurred with the data warehouse

endpoints.name: Get the user schema
endpoints.description: Returns the user schema of the workspace.

endpoints.name: Update the user schema
endpoints.description: Updates the user schema of the workspace.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: The new user schema. It must include at least one property.

parameters.name: primarySources
parameters.description:
The primary source for each schema property that has one, where the key is the property name and the value is the connection identifier.

This source defines where the definitive value for the property is read from, preventing other sources from overwriting it once it is set.

If no primary source is provided, the new schema will have no primary sources defined.

parameters.name: rePaths
parameters.description: The set of renamed and deleted paths. If a property path has been renamed in the new schema, `rePaths` maps the old path to the new one. If a property path has been removed, `rePaths` specifies the old path as the key with null as the value.

errors.message: alter schema operation is already in progress
identity resolution is currently in progress
data warehouse is in inspection mode
cannot update the schema as specified
error occurred with the data warehouse

endpoints.name: Preview a user schema update
endpoints.description:
Returns the SQL queries that would be executed on the warehouse to update the user schema.

It does not make any changes to the schema or execute any queries on the warehouse.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: The new user schema. It must include at least one property.

parameters.name: rePaths
parameters.description: The set of renamed and deleted paths. If a property path has been renamed in the new schema, `rePaths` maps the old path to the new one. If a property path has been removed, `rePaths` specifies the old path as the key with null as the value.

parameters.name: queries
parameters.description: The SQL queries that would be executed on the warehouse to modify the user schema.

errors.message: cannot update the schema as specified
error occurred with the data warehouse

resources.name: Warehouse
resources.description: A workspace enables Meergo to retrieve customer and event data from an external source location or send them to an external destination location.

endpoints.name: Get the warehouse
endpoints.description: Returns the name and the settings of the current workspace's warehouse.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the data warehouse.

parameters.name: settings
parameters.description: The settings of the data warehouse.

endpoints.name: Update the warehouse
endpoints.description: Updates the warehouse of the current workspace.

parameters.name: settings
parameters.description: The settings of the data warehouse.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: The mode of the data warehouse.

parameters.name: cancelIncompatibleOperations
parameters.description: Indicates whether operations currently running on the warehouse that are incompatible with the passed `mode` must be canceled.

errors.message: data warehouse is a different data warehouse
data warehouse settings are not valid
error occurred with the data warehouse

endpoints.name: Test the warehouse update
endpoints.description:
Tests the update of the warehouse of the current workspace.

If the settings are incorrect or the warehouse can’t be accessed with the given settings, an error will be returned. If no error occurs, the settings are valid.

parameters.name: settings
parameters.description: The settings of the data warehouse.

errors.message: data warehouse is a different data warehouse
data warehouse settings are not valid
error occurred with the data warehouse

endpoints.name: Update warehouse mode
endpoints.description: Updates the mode of the current workspace's data warehouse.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: The mode of the data warehouse.

parameters.name: cancelIncompatibleOperations
parameters.description: Indicates whether operations currently running on the warehouse that are incompatible with the passed `mode` must be canceled.

endpoints.name: Repair the warehouse
endpoints.description:
Repairs the current workspace's warehouse.

This method must be called when no identity resolution and alter schema operations are running on the data warehouse.

errors.message: error occurred with the data warehouse

endpoints.name: List warehouse types
endpoints.description: Returns a list of warehouse types than can be used for a workspace warehouse.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the warehouse type

properties.name: icon
properties.description: The SVG icon of the warehouse type.

resources.name: Workspaces
resources.description: Workspaces enable Meergo to retrieve customer and event data from an external source location or send them to an external destination location.

endpoints.name: Create a workspace
endpoints.description: Creates a new workspace.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The workspace's name.

parameters.name: userSchema
parameters.description: The user schema, defining the structure and properties of a user in the customer model of the workspace.

parameters.name: displayedProperties

properties.name: image
properties.description: The property name in the user schema that represents the user's image, in base64 format.

properties.name: firstName
properties.description: The property name in the user schema for the user's first name.

properties.name: lastName
properties.description: The property name in the user schema for the user's last name.

properties.name: information
properties.description: The property name in the user schema for additional user information. For example, the user schema property that represents the email.

parameters.description: The user properties to be displayed in the user profile within the UI. If any of these fields are empty, no corresponding value will be shown in the UI.

parameters.name: privacyRegion
parameters.description:
The privacy region specifies the geographic region that an app connection within this workspace should use for storing and managing user data. This helps ensure compliance with data protection regulations and user privacy standards.

However, a connection is not strictly required to use this region if it does not support it, or if your app account is tied to a different region. For more details, refer to the documentation of the specific connection's [connector](/connectors/) and the app documentation.

parameters.name: warehouse

properties.name: type
properties.description: The data warehouse type.

properties.name: mode
properties.description: The mode of the data warehouse.

properties.name: settings
properties.description: The settings of the data warehouse.

parameters.description:
The data warehouse of the workspace, serving as the central repository for the workspace's user data and collected events.

As part of the workspace provisioning process, the specified database is initialized with the schema, including all necessary tables, views, and stored procedures. The target database must be empty prior to initialization.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the workspace.

errors.message: data warehouse settings are not valid
error occurred with the data warehouse

endpoints.name: Test workspace creation
endpoints.description: Checks the process of creating a new workspace. It performs all the validations required for workspace creation, including verifying that the data warehouse can be initialized, without making any changes to the data warehouse or actually creating the workspace.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The workspace's name.

parameters.name: userSchema
parameters.description: The user schema, defining the structure and properties of a user in the customer model of the workspace.

parameters.name: displayedProperties

properties.name: image
properties.description: The property name in the user schema that represents the user's image, in base64 format.

properties.name: firstName
properties.description: The property name in the user schema for the user's first name.

properties.name: lastName
properties.description: The property name in the user schema for the user's last name.

properties.name: information
properties.description: The property name in the user schema for additional user information. For example, the user schema property that represents the email.

parameters.description: The user properties to be displayed in the user profile within the UI. If any of these fields are empty, no corresponding value will be shown in the UI.

parameters.name: privacyRegion
parameters.description:
The privacy region specifies the geographic region that an app connection within this workspace should use for storing and managing user data. This helps ensure compliance with data protection regulations and user privacy standards.

However, a connection is not strictly required to use this region if it does not support it, or if your app account is tied to a different region. For more details, refer to the documentation of the specific connection's [connector](/connectors/) and the app documentation.

parameters.name: warehouse

properties.name: type
properties.description: The data warehouse type.

properties.name: mode
properties.description: The mode of the data warehouse.

properties.name: settings
properties.description: The settings of the data warehouse.

parameters.description:
The data warehouse of the workspace, serving as the central repository for the workspace's user data and collected events.

Since this method only tests the creation of a workspace, the data warehouse is not modified. The target database must be empty to make the check succeed.

errors.message: data warehouse settings are not valid
error occurred with the data warehouse

endpoints.name: Update the workspace
endpoints.description: Updates the current workspace.

parameters.name: name

parameters.name: privacyRegion
parameters.description:
The privacy region specifies the geographic region that an app connection within this workspace should use for storing and managing user data. This helps ensure compliance with data protection regulations and user privacy standards.

However, a connection is not strictly required to use this region if it does not support it, or if your app account is tied to a different region. For more details, refer to the documentation of the specific connection's [connector](/connectors/) and the app documentation.

properties.name: image

properties.name: firstName

properties.name: lastName
properties.description: The property name in the user schema for the user's last name.

properties.description: The property name in the user schema for additional user information. For example, the user schema property that represents the email.

endpoints.name: List all workspaces
endpoints.description: Lists all the workspaces of the organization.

parameters.name: workspaces

properties.name: id
properties.description: The ID of the workspace.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The workspace's name.

properties.name: userSchema
properties.description: The user schema, defining the structure and properties of a user in the customer model of the workspace.

properties.name: userPrimarySources
properties.description:
The primary source for each user schema property that has one, where the key is the property name and the value is the connection identifier.

This source defines where the definitive value for the property is read from, preventing other sources from overwriting it once it is set.

properties.name: resolveIdentitiesOnBatchImport
properties.description:
Indicates whether identity resolution should be performed immediately after a batch import.

If set to true, Meergo will automatically resolve identities as soon as the import is finished.
If set to false, identity resolution will not happen automatically and will need to be triggered separately.

properties.name: identifiers
properties.description:
The user schema properties used to determine if two users are the same. The identity resolution checks these identities in order.
Two users are considered the same if they have the same value for the first identity, or if neither user has a value for the first identity, the identity resolution moves to the next one, and so on.

If no identifiers are specified, once identity resolution is performed, all users will be considered different from each other.

properties.name: mode
properties.description: The mode of the data warehouse.

properties.name: privacyRegion
properties.description:
The privacy region specifies the geographic region that an app connection within this workspace should use for storing and managing user data. This helps ensure compliance with data protection regulations and user privacy standards.

However, a connection is not strictly required to use this region if it does not support it, or if your app account is tied to a different region. For more details, refer to the documentation of the specific connection's [connector](/connectors/) and the app documentation.

properties.name: displayedProperties
image
properties.description: The property name in the user schema that represents the user's image, in base64 format.

properties.name: firstName
properties.description: The property name in the user schema for the user's first name.

properties.name: lastName
properties.description: The property name in the user schema for the user's last name.

properties.name: information
properties.description: The property name in the user schema for additional user information. For example, the user schema property that represents the email.
The user properties to be displayed in the user profile within the UI. If any of these fields are empty, no corresponding value will be shown in the UI.

parameters.description: The workspaces of the organization.
